topic: Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry
change_summary: detail_evidence_length grew from 856 to 1365 (+59.5%); detail_verdict_length grew from 1078 to 1196 (+10.9%); monitoring tag flipped 'hardcoded' -> 'none'. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-09T01:10Z. obe_skipped=5 (WIN-063/066/043/006/033 already re-reviewed since audit). Patches address c3 holes #1 (internal contradiction), #2 (monitoring tag), #4 (152/208 quantitative), #5 (post-hoc reframing), #6 (NOAA link), #7 (agonic positions). Cross-WIN check confirms boilerplate phrase removed from all 16 c3-flagged monitoring=none WINs (hole #3).
our_argument_summary: Verdict remains Refuted by Data. Patches since c3 substantially close the c3 hole list. Three residual issues remain: (1) the '180 degree symmetry as predicted by dome geometry' framing in detail_evidence is our characterization, not anchored to dome wording -- raw-text/02-wins.txt only has the one-line title 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' and never specifies 180; (2) the claim '15.6% deviation that exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere' is unanchored -- we should cite WIN-029's 5.3% as the comparator the dome itself uses; (3) the western agonic position is given as '~80W' which is outdated by the 2020s WMM (now further west). None change the verdict; all are minor sharpening opportunities.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. WIN-028 has no monitor script, no validation logic, no data-fetch line, and no hardcoded numerical target. raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 has only the one-line title 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED'. raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44 records the v50.4 (2026-03-09) addition. The dome's repository at this snapshot does not include monitor.py -- confirming no code-side validation for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Pure static folklore assertion. No agonic-line position calculation, no insurance-rate retrieval, no geomagnetic comparison, no anomaly-rate ingestion, no longitude-separation computation.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: RESOLVED. The c3 internal-contradiction (boilerplate paragraph asserting 'monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line' for a WIN with no monitor script) is gone. Current detail_verdict_text correctly states 'No monitor.py entry exists for this WIN; there is no script and no timestamped numerical prediction to evaluate.' This is now factually consistent with the structural reality and with the corrected monitoring='none' tag.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-028 does not appear in any monitor.py domain -- no monitor.py file exists in raw-text/ at this snapshot, and grep across raw-text/ for 'bermuda', 'agonic', 'devil' returns only the WIN-028 title line in 02-wins.txt and the v50.4 evolution-log entry. Site lists it only as a one-line confirmed WIN with no monitoring, validation, or data fetch. Tag was corrected from 'hardcoded' (per c3 hole #2 recommendation) -- verified in current data/wins.json.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Bermuda Triangle / Devil's Sea 'disappearance zone' framing is paranormal folklore, not a standard physics claim being relabeled. Agonic lines are real geophysics but their existence is not what the dome relabels -- the dome asserts a novel (and incorrect) folklore-to-geomagnetism correlation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Bermuda Triangle legend: Gaddis 1964, Berlitz 1974. Devil's Sea legend: Berlitz 1974. Agonic line positions: mapped since the 17th century (Halley 1701). Adopted in dome v50.4 (2026-03-09) per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44. Pure retrodiction of decades- to centuries-old material.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site connects disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, or moon_altitude=2534 km to the longitudes of Bermuda or Devil's Sea. Even the '180 symmetry' specification appears nowhere in raw-text/ -- only 'Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' is published. The 180 figure is our review's characterization, not derived from dome parameters.

holes_found.description: The '180 symmetry as predicted by dome geometry' framing in detail_evidence is our characterization, not a sourced dome quote. raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 contains only 'WIN-028 -- Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' -- no specification of 180, no specification of antipodal symmetry, no derivation from dome geometry. The 180 figure was carried forward from c1/c2 reviews and embedded in our detail_claim, but it cannot be sourced to the dome's actual published text. A fair-minded dome defender can correctly say: 'You attribute 180 to me; I never said 180. I said geometric symmetry, meaning two roughly opposite-hemisphere zones -- 152 is more than 40% of the planet east-to-west, qualifying as roughly opposite by any plain reading.' The c3 advocate-mode preemptive recommendation #2 said to 'explicitly quote the dome's exact 180 symmetry wording from the source page before computing 152/208 -- that anchors the strawman charge,' and that fix has NOT actually been applied -- the current text just asserts 180 as the dome's framing without sourcing it.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) find and quote a specific dome-site location that does specify 180 (worth checking the predictions page or older raw-text snapshots before claiming the dome made this specific commitment), OR (b) reframe the rebuttal to handle the dome's actual vagueness directly: 'The dome publishes only Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED with no quantitative tolerance. We test the claim under both plausible readings: as exact 180 antipodal symmetry, the regions miss by 28 (15.6% deviation, larger than confirmation thresholds the dome cites elsewhere -- e.g., WIN-029: 5.3% match cited as confirmation); as merely roughly-opposite-hemisphere, the dome's vague phrasing is unfalsifiable -- any pair of folklore zones in opposite hemispheres would qualify, which is too lax to count as a prediction.' Option (b) preempts the strawman charge and is the safer fix without source-hunting.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence claim '15.6% deviation that exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere' is unanchored. No specific WIN is named, no specific tolerance threshold is quoted. A defender will ask 'which thresholds?' Without an anchor, the comparison sounds like an unsupported assertion -- exactly the kind of quantitative hand-wave we accuse the dome of. The c3 advocate-mode preempt explicitly suggested anchoring on WIN-029's 5.3% (verified in raw-text/02-wins.txt:114 as 'H = c/(4x7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model'), but the current text doesn't cite it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere' with 'larger than the dome's own confirming thresholds -- WIN-029 cites a 5.3% match (raw-text/02-wins.txt:114) as confirmation in another quantitative WIN.' This anchors the cross-WIN tolerance comparison to an actual dome-published number.

holes_found.description: Western agonic line position '~80W' in detail_evidence is dated. As of 2025 WMM the western agonic line in the Atlantic has migrated to roughly 95-100W (the line was near 80W in the early 1990s and has drifted ~10/decade westward due to secular variation). Bermuda is at ~65W, so the current western agonic is now ~30-35 west of Bermuda -- an even larger gap than the c3 review's '~80W only grazes' framing implies. Note this is a strengthening opportunity, not a refutation: the more current the agonic position, the further from Bermuda it is.
holes_found.recommendation: Update to: 'Neither agonic line passes through its associated region. The western agonic line, currently near ~95-100W (per IGRF/WMM 2025), runs through the central United States -- well west of the Bermuda Triangle's eastern boundary at ~65W. The eastern agonic at ~100-120E lies 17-37 west of the Devil's Sea (137E).' Optional: add the secular-variation note that agonic lines have migrated 10/decade westward in the Atlantic, which itself contradicts a static dome geometry.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence ('The dome's claimed Bermuda/Japan symmetry has no factual basis') is slightly imprecise: the dome claim has multiple parts (folklore correlation + geometric symmetry + agonic-line correspondence). 'No factual basis' is a fair shorthand for the conclusion but glosses the multi-part structure. Not misleading enough to flag as major -- TLDR readers get the right verdict -- but a sentence on what specifically is wrong would be tighter.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: Replace with 'The dome's Bermuda/Japan symmetry claim fails on three independent grounds: NOAA/Lloyd's/USCG say there are no anomalous loss rates; the regions aren't actually 180 apart (152 short-arc); and the relevant agonic lines don't pass through either region.' This costs ~50 chars and gives a more rigorous TLDR.

stronger_arguments.description: Agonic-line secular variation is itself a falsifier of static dome geometry. The western agonic line was near 80W in the 1990s, ~95W by 2020, and near 100W now (IGRF/WMM models 1990-2025). A static cavity geometry (disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km -- fixed in dome parameters) cannot produce a time-varying agonic line. The very phenomenon the dome claims is geometric is in fact dynamic. This is positive evidence AGAINST the cavity-geometry interpretation, beyond merely 'the agonic line doesn't pass through Bermuda right now.'
sources: WMM 2020 / WMM 2025 declination grids (NOAA/NCEI/British Geological Survey)

stronger_arguments.description: If 152 counts as 'symmetry,' the dome claim is unfalsifiable. What longitude separation would NOT count as confirming dome geometry? 100? 60? The dome publishes no tolerance. The sole dome WIN where a precise tolerance is cited (WIN-029, 5.3%) suggests the dome treats single-digit-percent matches as confirming -- making the 15.6% deviation here far outside dome-internal precision norms. This is both a quantitative argument and an unfalsifiability argument.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that there are roughly two principal agonic loci on Earth (raw-text/02-wins.txt:113). Agonic lines (zero magnetic declination) are real geomagnetic features that historically had practical navigational significance -- Halley first mapped them in 1701, and 18th-19th century mariners used them as zero-correction reference lines. The dome's structural intuition that 'two notable points exist on opposite sides of the planet' is geographically defensible at coarse resolution.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing that agonic lines exist (data/wins.json:028 detail_evidence cites IGRF/CHAOS-7) does not justify (i) attaching them to Bermuda/Devil's-Sea folklore which has zero evidence base per NOAA/Lloyd's/USCG (data/wins.json:028 detail_evidence opening sentences), (ii) treating their separation as a meaningful 'symmetry' when it is 152 short-arc / 208 long-arc and 28 off 180 (quantitative_verification below), (iii) ignoring that agonic positions migrate westward over decades (a static cavity cannot produce that), or (iv) treating two folklore regions both pre-dating the dome model by decades (Bermuda: Gaddis 1964 / Berlitz 1974; Devil's Sea: Berlitz 1974) as a prospective prediction (post_hoc=true tag is correct).

deeper_analysis:
WIN-028 has improved substantially since c3. The c3 finding I'd been most worried about -- the cross-WIN boilerplate paragraph that asserted 'the monitor script hardcodes the target number' for 16 monitoring=none WINs (a class-level templated-debunk pattern that an attentive defender could quote-screenshot across pages) -- has been cleaned up: a grep across all 71 WINs for 'monitor script hardcodes the target number' returns 0 hits in monitoring=none WINs, and the phrase now appears only in 8 WINs whose monitoring is 'hardcoded' or 'live_fetch' where the boilerplate is structurally appropriate. That's a substantive site-wide fix.

The c3-recommended specific fixes for WIN-028 are also applied: (i) the internal-contradiction boilerplate is gone from detail_verdict_text and replaced with WIN-028-specific text correctly noting 'No monitor.py entry exists for this WIN'; (ii) monitoring tag corrected from 'hardcoded' to 'none'; (iii) the 152/208 arithmetic plus 28/15.6% deviation now appears explicitly in detail_evidence; (iv) the NOAA link is now the specific Bermuda Triangle page (oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/bermudatri.html) rather than the generic homepage; (v) post-hoc paragraph reframed temporally rather than via numerical-prediction vocabulary; (vi) agonic-line positions explicitly stated. The verdict remains Refuted by Data and is solidly correct.

The residual concerns are sharpening opportunities, not refutations:

1. The '180' specification cannot be sourced. Our text says 'as predicted by dome geometry' but raw-text/02-wins.txt:113 has only 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED' -- the dome publishes no tolerance, no antipodal angle, no derivation from disc_radius/firmament_height. A defender can correctly call this our characterization. The c3 advocate-mode preempt #2 anticipated exactly this and said 'quote the dome's exact 180 wording before computing 152/208' -- but that fix wasn't actually applied. We should either find a sourceable dome statement of 180 or reframe to test both readings (exact 180 fails by 28; 'roughly antipodal' is unfalsifiable).

2. The tolerance-threshold comparison ('exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere') is unanchored. WIN-029's 5.3% (raw-text/02-wins.txt:114) is the obvious dome-internal comparator; we should cite it.

3. Agonic-line position currency. '80W' is the early-1990s figure; current (2025 WMM) position is ~95-100W. Updating strengthens the rebuttal because the current position is even further from Bermuda. The secular-variation argument (lines migrate 10/decade westward) is itself a falsifier of static cavity geometry -- a stronger argument than 'the line doesn't currently pass through.'

Note on cross-WIN consistency: WIN-029 (Schumann conductive ceiling) is currently verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review. If we cite its '5.3% match' as a dome-internal confirming threshold to attack WIN-028, we should be careful not to imply we accept WIN-029's 5.3% as legitimate confirmation -- we don't, we Self-Contradict it. The framing should be 'on the dome's own self-described standards, 5.3% confirms; 15.6% therefore disconfirms by the same standard. The dome cannot have it both ways.' That's an internal-consistency attack, not an endorsement.

Other-side check (per operator preference). The strongest reading FOR the dome on what's left: 'You concede there are two principal agonic loci. You concede they are in opposite hemispheres. You concede agonic lines are physically real. The 152 you compute is more than 40% of the planet east-to-west -- that is opposite-hemisphere by any reasonable English reading of symmetry. Your insistence on 180 is a strawman.' Our text needs to either source 180 or explicitly handle this loose reading. The fix is hole #1 above; with that fix in place, the defense survives at rating 2 (preempted by the unfalsifiability counter-argument). Without it, the defense survives at rating 3-4 because the strawman charge is technically correct.

The verdict (Refuted by Data) is right. The recommended_action is minor_edit (sharpen 180 framing + anchor tolerance + update agonic position). No action needed on monitoring tag, post-hoc reframing, NOAA link, or cross-WIN boilerplate -- those c3 items are closed.

152/208 longitude arithmetic -- recomputed in Lens C, confirms detail_evidence
WIN-028 added in v50.4 (2026-03-09) per raw-text/06-evolution.txt:44
citations_failed: '180 geometric symmetry as predicted by dome geometry' -- phrase appears in our detail_evidence but cannot be sourced to raw-text/. Grep across raw-text/*.txt for '180', 'antipodal', 'symmetric', 'opposite hemisphere' returns no dome-side commitment to 180 in WIN-028's context. The only '180' result in raw-text is in the SAA WIN's 'Registered 2026-03-06' context, unrelated to WIN-028.
citations_unchecked: IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 / WMM 2025 declination grids -- not specifically DOI'd, just model names. Sufficient for a prose rebuttal but a more rigorous citation could note specific NOAA/NCEI URLs.
'15.6% deviation that exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere' -- anchor not provided in current text (see hole #2).

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am the dome author. I read the funwithscience review's WIN-028 page after their May 2026 patches. I observe: they corrected the monitoring tag (good). They removed the boilerplate paragraph that misdescribed my code (good). They added quantitative arithmetic to detail_evidence (152 vs 180, 15.6% deviation). But they still attribute '180 geometric symmetry as predicted by dome geometry' to me -- and I never said 180. My published wording is 'Bermuda Triangle / East Japan Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED.' I never specified an angle, never specified a tolerance, never claimed antipodal precision. Their 152 vs 180 calculation refutes a precision I didn't claim. 152 is more than 40% of the planet east-to-west -- by any plain English reading of 'symmetry,' two zones separated by 152 on the short arc are in opposite hemispheres. My claim is that two notable folklore zones happen to coincide with the two principal agonic-line loci, which the reviewers themselves concede exist on roughly opposite sides of the planet. The reviewers' own quantitative argument refutes a strawman version of my claim, not the actual claim. I would also note: their assertion that 15.6% 'exceeds the model's own tolerance thresholds elsewhere' is unsourced -- they don't say which threshold, from which WIN, in which paragraph. That's a hand-waved comparison from people who criticize me for hand-waving.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two text changes neutralize this defense fully: (1) Reframe detail_evidence to 'The dome publishes Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED with no specified tolerance. Tested under both plausible readings: as exact 180 antipodal symmetry, the regions miss by 28 (15.6% deviation, larger than the 5.3% confirmation match the dome cites for WIN-029 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:114); as merely roughly-opposite-hemisphere, the dome's vague phrasing is unfalsifiable -- any pair of folklore zones in different hemispheres would qualify.' This addresses both the strawman charge AND the unanchored-tolerance charge in one move. (2) Optionally source the 180 figure if a dome-side commitment to it exists in earlier raw-text snapshots or the predictions page; if so, quote it directly. The dual-reading frame works either way and is the safer fix because it doesn't depend on source-hunting.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No inconsistencies introduced by this c4 review. WIN-029 (Schumann conductive ceiling, 5.3% match cited as confirmation) is the natural cross-WIN comparator for an internal-tolerance attack on WIN-028. WIN-029's overall verdict in our review is Self-Contradicted, which means we don't accept its 5.3% match as legitimate confirmation. The cross-WIN argument we should make for WIN-028 is internal-consistency, not endorsement: 'on the dome's own self-described standard, 5.3% confirms; 15.6% therefore disconfirms.' WIN-027 (Southern Distance Quadratic, R^2=0.7874) uses a different framework (R^2-based fit goodness) and is not directly tolerance-comparable. WIN-064 (SAA cell asymmetry, 3:1 ratio cited as structurally incompatible with symmetric dipole) uses a different asymmetry framing. Cross-WIN consistency holds for the c4 patches; the residual hole #1 (180 framing) is a within-WIN issue, not a cross-WIN one. NOTE: the c3 cross-WIN finding (boilerplate template across 26 WINs) has been substantially closed -- verified by grep across data/wins.json for 'monitor script hardcodes the target number' returning zero hits in monitoring=none WINs.

claims_checked: Longitude separation between Bermuda Triangle (~25N, 71W) and Devil's Sea (~25N, 137E)
Short-arc and long-arc separations and deviation from 180
quantitative_verification.computations: Bermuda center: -71 (West). Devil's Sea center: +137 (East). Eastward separation = (137 - (-71)) mod 360 = 208. Westward / short-arc = 360 - 208 = 152. Deviation from 180: |180 - 152| = 28 (or |208 - 180| = 28 going east). Percentage deviation = 28/180 = 15.555%. Recomputed via Python and confirmed: 152 short, 208 long, 28 absolute deviation, 15.56% relative. This matches detail_evidence exactly. The c2 framing used '208 separation' (long arc); c3 disclosed both arcs; c4 detail_evidence text now uses '152 short arc, 208 long arc, 28 off, 15.6%' which is the most rigorous form.
